segments.text: Lesson 29.

translations.zh-Hans: 第29课。

segments.text: The Hovercraft.

translations.zh-Hans: 气垫船。

segments.text: First listen and then answer the following question.

translations.zh-Hans: 先听录音，然后回答下面的问题。

segments.text: What is a hovercraft riding on when it is in motion?

translations.zh-Hans: 气垫船行驶时承托在什么上面？

segments.text: Many strange new means of transport have been developed in our century, the strangest of them being perhaps the hovercraft.

translations.zh-Hans: 本世纪发展出许多奇特的新型交通工具，其中最奇特的也许就是气垫船。

segments.text: In 1953, a former electronics engineer in his fifties, Christopher Cockerrell, who had turned to boat building on the Norfolk Broads, suggested an idea on which he had been working for many

translations.zh-Hans: 1953年，年过五旬的前电子工程师克里斯托弗·科克雷尔已转而在诺福克湖区造船。他把自己研究多

segments.text: years to the British government and industrial circles.

translations.zh-Hans: 年的一个构想提交给英国政府和工业界。

segments.text: It was the idea of supporting a craft on a pad or cushion of low pressure air, ringed with a curtain of higher pressure air.

translations.zh-Hans: 这个构想是用低压空气垫承托船体，并在周围形成一道高压空气幕。

segments.text: Ever since, people have had difficulty in deciding whether the craft should be ranged among ships, planes, or land vehicles, for it is something in between a boat and an aircraft.

translations.zh-Hans: 从那以后，人们一直难以决定应把它归入船舶、飞机还是陆地车辆，因为它介于船和飞机之间。

segments.text: As a shipbuilder, Cockerrell was trying to find a solution to the problem of the wave resistance which wastes a good deal of a surface ship's power and limits its speed.

translations.zh-Hans: 身为造船者，科克雷尔试图解决波浪阻力问题；这种阻力会浪费水面船只的大量动力，并限制其速度。

segments.text: His answer was to lift the vessel out of the water by making it ride on a cushion of air, no more than one or two feet thick.

translations.zh-Hans: 他的办法是让船体升离水面，行驶在仅一两英尺厚的气垫上。

segments.text: This is done by a great number of ring-shaped air jets on the bottom of the craft.

translations.zh-Hans: 这是通过船底大量环形喷气口实现的。

segments.text: It flies, therefore, but it cannot fly higher.

translations.zh-Hans: 因此它确实在“飞”，却不能飞得更高。

segments.text: Its action depends on the surface, water or ground over which it rides.

translations.zh-Hans: 它的运行依赖其掠过的水面或地面。

segments.text: The first tests on the Solent in 1959 caused a sensation.

translations.zh-Hans: 1959年在索伦特海峡进行的首次试验引起轰动。

segments.text: The hovercraft traveled first over the water, then mounted the beach, climbed up the dunes, and set down on a road.

translations.zh-Hans: 气垫船先驶过水面，随后冲上海滩、爬上沙丘，最后停在公路上。

segments.text: Later, it crossed the channel, riding smoothly over the waves, which presented no problem.

translations.zh-Hans: 后来它穿越英吉利海峡，平稳掠过毫不构成障碍的波浪。

segments.text: Since that time, various types of hovercraft have appeared and taken up regular service.

translations.zh-Hans: 此后，各种类型的气垫船相继问世并投入定期运营。

segments.text: The hovercraft is particularly useful in large areas with poor communications such as Africa or Australia.

translations.zh-Hans: 气垫船在非洲或澳大利亚等交通不便的广阔地区尤其有用。

segments.text: It can become a flying fruit bowl, carrying bananas from the plantations to the ports.

translations.zh-Hans: 它可以变成一只“会飞的水果盘”，把香蕉从种植园运到港口。

segments.text: Giant hovercraft liners could span the Atlantic, and the railway of the future may well be the hover train riding on its air cushion over a single rail, which it never touches, at speeds up to three hundred miles

translations.zh-Hans: 巨型气垫客轮可以横渡大西洋；未来的铁路很可能是气垫列车，以每小时300英里

segments.text: per hour.

translations.zh-Hans: 的速度在一条从不接触的单轨上方靠气垫行驶。

segments.text: The possibilities appear unlimited.

translations.zh-Hans: 其可能性似乎无穷无尽。
